_meta.provenance: each card's issue state/date verified live via `gh issue view`. Status as of 2026-07-01: 1 verified (RMD-XARRAY-0001, affected 2023.4.0 reproduced in isolated venv + fix confirmed on current 2024.11.0), 4 reproducible (numpy/scipy/pandas/networkx OPEN defects reproduced on current versions, scripts in code/realdefects/repro/), 4 candidate (CLOSED, need old versions; Python 3.12 lacks old wheels so those await isolated-venv reproduction). Version fields marked (unconfirmed) where the exact release was not checked out and run.
_meta.status_legend: candidate = URL + behavioral evidence exist, MR violation NOT yet reproduced locally; reproducible = MR violation reproduced by a committed script; verified = full chain (affected+fixed version/commit + repro script + MR oracle).
_meta.boundary: This corpus targets general scientific-Python libraries (function-level SUTs), which differs from the pinned paper's SciML-surrogate SUTs. See execution-log s0.4 fork. Not injected into the manuscript.

cards.fixed_version_or_commit: closed COMPLETED 2021-11-12; fix targeted 1.22.0 (exact commit unconfirmed)
cards.behavioral_symptom: np.einsum on float16 arrays returns all-zeros instead of the correct contraction, from numpy 1.21.0
cards.source_input_x: x float16 array with a nonzero contraction, e.g. a=np.ones((5,5),dtype=float16); np.einsum('ij->', a)
cards.transformed_input_Tx: T(x) = same einsum on x.astype(float32) (or float64), i.e. compute in a higher dtype then compare
cards.expected_relation_R: np.einsum(expr, a.astype(float16)) approx-equals np.einsum(expr, a.astype(float32)) within float16 tolerance, and is NOT identically zero when the true contraction is nonzero
cards.violating_observation: float16 path returns 0 everywhere while the float32/float64 path returns the correct nonzero value
cards.why_not_crash_only: no exception is raised; the returned numeric value is wrong (all-zero), a clean value-level violation of the dtype-invariance MR
cards.reproduction_cost: low (pip install numpy==1.21.x, CPU, seconds) once an affected wheel is pinned
cards.sms_vs_ms_hypothesis: SMS catches this via a dtype-invariance MR; syntactic MS mutants (arithmetic/relational operator swaps in test code) rarely model a dtype-specific accumulation-buffer regression, so MS would over-credit a suite that never exercises float16

cards.behavioral_symptom: np.einsum('i...->i', x) with optimize=True returns array([18,18,18]) but optimize=False errors out on the same expression/array
cards.source_input_x: x = 3x3x3 int array; np.einsum('i...->i', x, optimize=True)
cards.transformed_input_Tx: T(x) = identical expression with optimize=False (the only changed knob is the optimization path)
cards.expected_relation_R: np.einsum(expr, x, optimize=True) equals np.einsum(expr, x, optimize=False); an optimization flag must not change the mathematical result
cards.violating_observation: optimize=True returns array([18,18,18]); optimize=False errors out on the same call -> the two equivalent evaluation paths disagree
cards.why_not_crash_only: not crash-only: the optimized path returns a definite correct value ([18,18,18]); the defect is the DISAGREEMENT between two paths that must be equivalent, detectable by a differential/optimization-equivalence MR (a stricter value-vs-value instance is the sibling einsum-optimize broadcasting bug family)
cards.reproduction_cost: low (pip, CPU, seconds); reproduced on numpy 2.5.0
cards.sms_vs_ms_hypothesis: SMS catches this with an optimization-invariance MR (compare optimize on/off); MS's syntactic mutants operate inside one evaluation path and cannot express cross-configuration equivalence, so MS is blind to optimization-path divergences

cards.affected_version: reproduced on scipy 1.18.0 (OPEN; unconfirmed lower bound)
cards.behavioral_symptom: signal.fftconvolve/oaconvolve with a broadcast batch dim and axes=1 collapse the batch dimension to 1 for mode='same', while mode='valid'/'full' preserve it
cards.source_input_x: x shape (1,100), k shape (7,10); fftconvolve(x, k, axes=1, mode='valid') -> shape (7,91), batch dim B=7 preserved
cards.transformed_input_Tx: T = same x, k with mode='same' (only the mode changes; broadcasting/axes identical)
cards.expected_relation_R: the broadcast batch dimension result.shape[0] is preserved across modes: mode='same' must keep B=7 exactly as mode='valid'/'full' do
cards.violating_observation: mode='same' returns shape (1,100) (batch dim collapsed to 1) while 'valid'/'full' return (7,91)/(7,109) with B=7 preserved
cards.why_not_crash_only: no exception; the output has the wrong shape (batch dim dropped), a structural MR violation across modes. CORRECTED after local reproduction on scipy 1.18.0: fftconvolve and oaconvolve AGREE with each other (both (1,100)); the violated relation is cross-MODE broadcasting consistency, NOT cross-method equivalence
cards.reproduction_cost: low (pip, CPU, seconds); reproduced on scipy 1.18.0
cards.sms_vs_ms_hypothesis: SMS catches this with a cross-mode broadcasting-invariance MR; syntactic MS mutants do not encode 'batch dim preserved across modes', so MS over-credits a suite that tests only one convolution mode

cards.fixed_version_or_commit: closed COMPLETED 2022-04-26; fix in a 1.9.x release (exact version/commit unconfirmed)
cards.behavioral_symptom: signal.convolve gives different results for method='fft' vs method='direct' when inputs contain INF/NAN (fft path spreads a single NaN across the whole output)
cards.source_input_x: arrays a, b with a single special value; scipy.signal.convolve(a, b, method='direct')
cards.transformed_input_Tx: T = same a, b via scipy.signal.convolve(a, b, method='fft')
cards.expected_relation_R: convolve(method='direct') and convolve(method='fft') should agree on the non-special entries; method must not change which output positions are finite
cards.violating_observation: fft path returns all-NaN output while direct path localizes the NaN, so the two methods disagree on finite positions
cards.why_not_crash_only: not crash-only: both return arrays; the defect is a value-level method disagreement (accepted as a real bug: scipy closed it COMPLETED). NOTE nuance: NaN propagation in FFT is partly expected FP behavior; the MR here is scoped to positions that should be finite under direct convolution
cards.sms_vs_ms_hypothesis: SMS catches this with a method-equivalence MR; MS syntactic mutants do not model FFT-vs-direct numerical-path divergence, so MS over-credits a suite testing only one method

cards.behavioral_symptom: df.groupby(...).resample(on=<col>) gives incorrect results when the DataFrame index is out of order, even though the 'on' column (not the index) defines the resampling
cards.source_input_x: df with a datetime column 'ts'; df.groupby('g').resample('D', on='ts').agg(...) with df sorted by index
cards.transformed_input_Tx: T(df) = df.sample(frac=1) (rows permuted / index shuffled), same groupby-resample-on-'ts'
cards.expected_relation_R: the aggregation result is invariant to row order because grouping/resampling keys come from columns, not the row index: agg(df) equals agg(permute(df)) up to canonical sort
cards.violating_observation: shuffling the index changes the aggregated output, violating row-order invariance
cards.why_not_crash_only: no exception; the aggregated numeric result is wrong under permutation, a clean value-level MR violation
cards.reproduction_cost: low (pip, CPU, seconds); reproduced on pandas 2.2.3
cards.sms_vs_ms_hypothesis: SMS catches this with a row-permutation-invariance MR; MS mutants (operator/constant swaps) inside pandas internals are unlikely to model an index-ordering coupling, so MS would credit a suite that only tests pre-sorted frames

cards.fixed_version_or_commit: closed COMPLETED 2020-04-24; fix in a 1.1.x release (exact version/commit unconfirmed)
cards.behavioral_symptom: groupby(...).transform(...) with observed=True ignores sort=False, so transform results are misaligned to the original (unsorted) rows
cards.source_input_x: df with a categorical group key; df.groupby('g', sort=False, observed=True).transform('mean')
cards.transformed_input_Tx: T = same computation with sort=True, then re-aligned to original row order (sort flag should not change per-row transform output)
cards.expected_relation_R: transform output is aligned to the input rows regardless of sort/observed: value at row i equals the group statistic of row i's group; sort flag must not permute the alignment
cards.violating_observation: with observed=True and sort=False, transform values are attached to the wrong rows
cards.why_not_crash_only: no exception; the per-row values are correct but misaligned, a value-placement MR violation
cards.sms_vs_ms_hypothesis: SMS catches this with a transform-alignment MR; MS syntactic mutants do not encode 'output must align to input rows', so MS over-credits a suite that tests only sorted or single-group frames

cards.fixed_version_or_commit: closed COMPLETED 2022-03-22; fix in a 1.1.x release (exact version/commit unconfirmed)
cards.behavioral_symptom: Ridge with sample_weight gives wrong results for sparse input, disagreeing with the equivalent dense fit
cards.source_input_x: X_dense (dense array), y, sample_weight; Ridge(...).fit(X_dense, y, sample_weight=w).coef_
cards.transformed_input_Tx: T(X) = scipy.sparse.csr_matrix(X_dense) (same data, sparse encoding); Ridge(...).fit(X_sparse, y, sample_weight=w).coef_
cards.expected_relation_R: coefficients from dense and sparse inputs are equal within tolerance: a sparse encoding is only a representation change and must not change the fitted model
cards.violating_observation: sparse fit with sample weights yields coefficients differing from the dense fit
cards.why_not_crash_only: no exception; the fitted numeric coefficients are wrong for sparse input, a value-level MR violation
cards.reproduction_cost: low-medium (pip install scikit-learn==1.0.x + control random_state, CPU, seconds)
cards.sms_vs_ms_hypothesis: SMS catches this with a sparse-dense equivalence MR; MS mutants inside the solver rarely model the representation-specific sample-weight path, so MS over-credits a suite that only tests dense inputs

cards.affected_version: reproduced on networkx 3.4.2 (fixed 2026-06-29; unconfirmed lower bound)
cards.behavioral_symptom: relabel_nodes with a node-merging (non-injective) mapping gives different node attributes for copy=True vs copy=False; the in-place path overwrites the surviving node's attributes with a merged node's
cards.source_input_x: graph G with node attributes (nodes 0..4, x=i,y=i); nx.relabel_nodes(G, {1:4, 2:4}, copy=True) -> node 4 keeps {x:4,y:4}
cards.transformed_input_Tx: T = same graph and mapping with copy=False (only the copy flag changes)
cards.expected_relation_R: copy=True and copy=False produce the same node attributes; the copy flag is a representation choice that must not change semantics
cards.violating_observation: copy=True gives node 4 = {x:4,y:4}; copy=False gives node 4 = {x:2,y:2} -- the two paths disagree. NOTE: a clean BIJECTIVE relabel preserves attributes correctly on 3.4.2; the defect manifests only under a node-merging mapping (corrected after local reproduction)
cards.why_not_crash_only: no exception; the in-place path yields wrong per-node attributes, a value-level copy-flag-invariance MR violation
cards.reproduction_cost: low (pip, CPU, seconds); reproduced on networkx 3.4.2
cards.sms_vs_ms_hypothesis: SMS catches this with a copy-flag-invariance MR on a merging relabel; MS syntactic mutants do not encode 'copy=True and copy=False must agree', so MS over-credits a suite that only relabels with copy=True

cards.affected_version: 2023.4.0 (reproduced in isolated venv: label correspondence scrambled, 'four'=506 vs expected 1, 'nine'=1 vs expected 506)
cards.fixed_version_or_commit: closed COMPLETED 2023-04-20; fix confirmed present in xarray 2024.11.0 (0 mismatches, live check)
cards.behavioral_symptom: groupby_bins with string labels attaches groups to the wrong labels (sorts by label name instead of bin lower edge) starting in 2023.4.0
cards.source_input_x: DataArray over coords; da.groupby_bins('coords', bins, labels=L).groups; count in group[label]
cards.transformed_input_Tx: T = pandas.cut(coords, bins, labels=L).value_counts() (the assignment groupby_bins uses internally) as the correctness oracle
cards.expected_relation_R: for each label, len(group[label]) equals the pandas.cut count for that label; the group attached to a label must contain exactly the coords whose bin carries that label
cards.violating_observation: on 2023.4.0 the per-label counts are permuted vs pandas.cut (e.g. 'four' got 506 but should be 1); on 2024.11.0 they match
cards.why_not_crash_only: no exception; the returned grouping carries wrong label counts, a value-correspondence MR violation
cards.reproduction_cost: low-medium (isolated venv: xarray==2023.4.0 + numpy==1.26.4 + pandas==2.1.4; old wheels do not build on py3.12 so affected side is recorded provenance)
cards.sms_vs_ms_hypothesis: SMS catches this with a label-correspondence MR; MS syntactic mutants do not encode 'label must match the bin', so MS over-credits a suite that checks only group counts, not label correspondence
